title: jacquiniella miserrima
side_effects: Jacquiniella miserrima can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in sensitive individuals. Ingesting the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils can lead to headaches and dizziness in some people, while excessive use can cause interactions with medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. In severe cases, improper use of Jacquiniella miserrima can result in anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
